title: Add Remember Me feature to login with persistent session

requirements.summary: Add a 'Remember Me' checkbox to the login/authentication UI that persists the user's preference in localStorage
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Remember Me' checkbox is visible on the login screen/AuthGate login prompt
Checking 'Remember Me' saves the preference to localStorage (e.g., key: 'rememberMe')
The checkbox state persists across page refreshes

file_operations.description: Add a 'Remember Me' checkbox to the login UI section within AuthGate. When the checkbox is checked, save the preference to localStorage using the key 'rememberMe' with a boolean value. On component mount, read from localStorage to initialize the checkbox state. Position the checkbox near the login button with clear labeling. Do not modify the underlying useInternetIdentity hook calls; only add UI and localStorage logic around the existing login flow.

requirements.summary: When 'Remember Me' is enabled, maintain the authenticated session on page load and show a confirmation dialog before sign-out
acceptanceCriteria: When 'Remember Me' is enabled, the user remains signed in after closing and reopening the browser tab
When 'Remember Me' is enabled, clicking the sign-out button shows a confirmation dialog before signing out
The confirmation dialog clearly warns the user that 'Remember Me' is enabled
If the user confirms sign-out, they are logged out and the 'Remember Me' preference is cleared from localStorage
If the user cancels the confirmation, they remain signed in
When 'Remember Me' is not enabled, sign-out behaves as normal without a confirmation dialog

file_operations.description: On component mount, check if 'rememberMe' is true in localStorage. If true and the user is not authenticated, attempt to restore the session by calling the login function from useInternetIdentity (Internet Identity may restore the session automatically if the user was previously authenticated). Ensure this logic runs only once on mount to avoid loops. Do not modify useInternetIdentity.ts directly; implement session restoration logic in AuthGate using the existing login function.

Modify the logout/sign-out button handler in the Layout component. Before calling the clear() function from useInternetIdentity, check if 'rememberMe' is true in localStorage. If true, show a confirmation dialog (use a shadcn/ui Dialog or AlertDialog component) with a message like 'Are you sure you want to sign out? Remember Me is enabled.' If the user confirms, proceed with clear(), clear the queryClient cache, and remove the 'rememberMe' key from localStorage. If the user cancels, do nothing and keep them signed in. If 'rememberMe' is false or not set, proceed with logout as normal without showing the dialog.
